name: Hike from Villaverde del Río – circular
name_original: Catillana desde Villaverde del Río – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villaverde Del Río traverse a diverse landscape, situated in the Vega del Guadalquivir region and at the base of the Sierra Morena foothills. The area features varied terrains, including fertile plains and orchards along the Guadalquivir and Viar rivers, alongside more mountainous sections with abundant vegetation. This geographical mix provides a range of gradients and scenic backdrops for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Villaverde Del Río The most popular touring cycling route is Hike from Villaverde del Río – circular, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete, offering an easy circular path through the local scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Villaverde del Río – Hermitage of the Divine Shepherdess loop from Villaverde del Río, a difficult 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path that climbs through the region's varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Villaverde del Río – Burguillos loop from Villaverde del Río, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail leading through the Sierra Morena foothills, often completed in about 5 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Villaverde Del Río is defined by riverside paths along the Guadalquivir, ascents into the Sierra Morena foothills, and routes through fertile plains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Villaverde Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaverde Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Villaverde Del Río?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Villaverde Del Río, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 175 times, providing an average rating of 4.3 stars.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Villaverde Del Río offers several easy touring cycling routes. For a gentle introduction to the area's scenery, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992991720/hike-from-villaverde-del-rio-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Villaverde del Río – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 28.5 km path. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantillana – Ermita de San Bartolomé loop from Cantillana&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 20 km route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Villaverde Del Río?
A: The terrain around Villaverde Del Río is quite diverse, ranging from fertile plains and orchards along the Guadalquivir and Viar rivers to more mountainous sections in the Sierra Morena foothills. You'll encounter a mix of flat riverside paths and challenging climbs, providing varied gradients and scenic backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Villaverde Del Río are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992991720/hike-from-villaverde-del-rio-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Villaverde del Río – circular&lt;/a&gt;, or for a more challenging option, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde del Río – Hermitage of the Divine Shepherdess loop from Villaverde del Río&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Vega del Guadalquivir region, especially from elevated points like Mesa Redonda. The area is also known for natural features such as Las Calderas, where the Siete Arroyos Stream creates waterfalls and pools. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4081545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Chaparral&lt;/a&gt;, a notable trail in the region.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical or cultural landmarks?
A: Yes, some routes may take you near historical and cultural sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde del Río – Hermitage of the Divine Shepherdess loop from Villaverde del Río&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Hermitage. Other points of interest in the wider area include the ruins of a medieval castle and the Shrine of Nuestra Señora de Aguas Santas. You can also find religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7373778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Immaculate Conception&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Villaverde Del Río?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which includes both challenging ascents into the Sierra Morena foothills and pleasant riverside paths along the Guadalquivir, offering varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde del Río – Burguillos loop from Villaverde del Río&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and distance, leading through the Sierra Morena foothills. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40310283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde del Río – Camino del Chaparral loop from Villaverde del Río&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villaverde Del Río?
A: While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling. Summers can be very hot, especially in the plains, while winters are generally mild. Always check local weather forecasts before your ride.

Q: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities besides cycling in Villaverde Del Río?
A: Yes, Villaverde Del Río is a hub for various outdoor activities. The Las Calderas area is recommended for hiking, horse riding, canyoning, and rappelling. The Guadalquivir River provides opportunities for fishing and canoeing. Mesa Redonda is also popular for hiking and climbing, offering stunning views.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the Guadalquivir River?
A: Yes, touring cycling around Villaverde Del Río is characterized by its riverside paths along the Guadalquivir. These routes offer a flatter, more relaxed cycling experience through fertile plains and orchards, showcasing the beauty of Andalusia's 'Rio Grande'.
tips: Building built in the 18th century on the remains of the previous hermitage, which, according to tradition, was built in the 7th century, in the place where the image of the Blessed Virgin appeared to the pastor Juan Bueno.

The Iglesia de la Purísima Concepción in Villaverde del Río is located on the Plaza Blas Infante and is a popular place for both locals and visitors. The church is of Baroque style and consists of a single, spacious nave closed by a beautiful plaster cross vault. The façade of the church is adintel and is flanked by pilasters that crown a pediment with a pinnacle. Inside the church is a neoclassical altarpiece in which the Purísima Concepción is venerated, along with portal altarpieces of Santa Rita and San Sebastián.
Remains of the old bridge over the Guadalquivir river. It was an arched bridge built in 1942.
